factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in Tools & Storage and Industrial segments.

evidence.text: Tools & Storage net sales increased 2% due to continued organic growth of 9%, driven by strong growth across all regions, partially offset by foreign currency pressure of 7%. Industrial net sales declined 7% compared to the third quarter of 2014 as a result of a 7% decrease due to foreign currency pressures. Organic growth was flat as positive organic growth in Engineered Fastening was offset by expected declines in Infrastructure volumes.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating in key segments despite some offsetting factors.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Volume and price increased 5% and 1%, respectively, but were more than offset by an 8% decrease due to foreign currency fluctuations. Tools & Storage net sales increased 2% due to continued organic growth of 9%, driven by strong growth across all regions, partially offset by foreign currency pressure of 7%.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully raising prices, contributing to revenue growth.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is improving, particularly in the Tools & Storage segment.

evidence.text: Organic sales increased 10% primarily due to organic growth of 13% in North America, 8% in Europe, and 4% in emerging markets. Negative impacts from foreign currency fluctuations resulted in a 7% decrease in net sales during the first nine months of 2015.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is improving significantly across regions.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in the Tools & Storage segment.

evidence.text: Tools & Storage net sales increased 2% due to continued organic growth of 9%, driven by strong growth across all regions, partially offset by foreign currency pressure of 7%. Volume and price increased 5% and 1%, respectively, but were more than offset by an 8% decrease due to foreign currency fluctuations.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in the Tools & Storage segment.

factors.summary: The fastest growing end markets are Tools & Storage and Industrial, particularly in North America and Europe.

evidence.text: Tools & Storage net sales increased 2% due to continued organic growth of 9%, driven by strong growth across all regions, partially offset by foreign currency pressure of 7%. Industrial net sales declined 7% compared to the third quarter of 2014 as a result of a 7% decrease due to foreign currency pressures. Organic growth was flat as positive organic growth in Engineered Fastening was offset by expected declines in Infrastructure volumes.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The fastest growing end markets are showing momentum in key regions.

factors.summary: Input costs are declining due to commodity deflation, which is helping to improve margins.

evidence.text: The revised EPS outlook reflects the impacts of a stronger operational performance combined with incremental commodity deflation which are expected to more than offset a slower growth outlook for the industrial portions of the Company's businesses and slightly higher currency headwinds, now estimated to be closer to the high end of the prior range of $200 - $220 million. The Company expects free cash flow for 2015 to approach $1 billion reflecting the impact of strong organic growth on working capital levels.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are declining due to commodity deflation, improving margins.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving due to volume leverage, price, productivity, and cost management.

evidence.text: Gross profit was $1.027 billion , or 36.3% of net sales, in the third quarter of 2015 compared to $1.047 billion , or 36.4% of net sales, in the third quarter of 2014 , as favorable volume leverage, price, productivity, cost actions and commodity deflation substantially offset unfavorable currency. Gross profit was $3.058 billion , or 36.7% of net sales, in the first nine months of 2015 compared to $3.052 billion , or 36.5% of net sales, in the first nine months of 2014. The year-over-year increase in the profit rate was primarily due to the same factors that impacted the third quarter as discussed above.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are improving due to various favorable factors.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, contributing to improved margins.

evidence.text: The decrease in the rate reflects the positive impacts of volume leverage and cost controls. On a year-to-date basis, SG&A, inclusive of the provision for doubtful accounts, was $1.876 billion , or 22.5% of net sales, in 2015 compared to $1.938 billion , or 23.2% of net sales, in 2014 , respectively. The year-over-year decrease in the rate was primarily due to the same factors that impacted the third quarter as discussed above.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, improving margins.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to cost management and operational efficiency initiatives.

evidence.text: The increase in the segment profit rate was driven by volume leverage, price, productivity, cost management and lower commodity prices, which more than offset significant currency pressures. The year-over-year increase in the profit rate was primarily due to the same factors that impacted the third quarter as discussed above.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates clear improvement in productivity due to cost management and operational efficiency.

factors.summary: Management has raised its forward guidance for EPS to $5.80 to $5.95 from $5.70 to $5.90.

evidence.text: The Company has raised its previously communicated guidance on diluted earnings per share to $5.80 to $5.95 from $5.70 to $5.90. The revised EPS outlook reflects the impacts of a stronger operational performance combined with incremental commodity deflation which are expected to more than offset a slower growth outlook for the industrial portions of the Company's businesses and slightly higher currency headwinds, now estimated to be closer to the high end of the prior range of $200 - $220 million.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Management has raised EPS guidance, reflecting confidence in operational performance and commodity deflation.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as evidenced by continued share buybacks and dividend increases.

evidence.text: The Company remains committed to returning approximately 50% of free cash flow to shareholders through a strong and growing dividend as well as opportunistically repurchasing shares. The remaining free cash flow (approximately 50%) will be deployed towards acquisitions. Since the beginning of the fourth quarter of 2014, the Company has reduced its share count by the equivalent of approximately $1.2 billion worth of shares by utilizing both cash and equity derivatives.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Share buybacks and dividend increases demonstrate strong confidence in the company's future.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include foreign currency fluctuations and slower growth in industrial markets.

evidence.text: The revised EPS outlook reflects the impacts of a stronger operational performance combined with incremental commodity deflation which are expected to more than offset a slower growth outlook for the industrial portions of the Company's businesses and slightly higher currency headwinds, now estimated to be closer to the high end of the prior range of $200 - $220 million. The Company expects free cash flow for 2015 to approach $1 billion reflecting the impact of strong organic growth on working capital levels.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign currency fluctuations and slower industrial growth pose risks, though partially offset by other factors.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on growth through acquisitions and organic expansion.

evidence.text: The Company is continuing to pursue a growth and acquisition strategy that involves industry, geographic and customer diversification to foster sustainable revenue, earnings and cash flow growth. The Company is focused on growing organically, including increasing its presence in emerging markets, with a goal of generating greater than 20% of annual revenues from those markets, and leveraging the Stanley Fulfillment System, a now expanded program focused on upgrading innovation and digital capabilities while maintaining commercial and supply chain excellence, and funding required investments through functional transformation.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear strategic focus on growth through acquisitions and organic expansion indicates a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Foreign currency fluctuations and commodity prices are key sensitivities impacting near-term results.

evidence.text: The revised EPS outlook reflects the impacts of a stronger operational performance combined with incremental commodity deflation which are expected to more than offset a slower growth outlook for the industrial portions of the Company's businesses and slightly higher currency headwinds, now estimated to be closer to the high end of the prior range of $200 - $220 million. The Company expects free cash flow for 2015 to approach $1 billion reflecting the impact of strong organic growth on working capital levels.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign currency fluctuations and commodity prices are key risks impacting near-term results.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are increasing through dividends and share buybacks.

evidence.text: The Company remains committed to returning approximately 50% of free cash flow to shareholders through a strong and growing dividend as well as opportunistically repurchasing shares. The remaining free cash flow (approximately 50%) will be deployed towards acquisitions. Since the beginning of the fourth quarter of 2014, the Company has reduced its share count by the equivalent of approximately $1.2 billion worth of shares by utilizing both cash and equity derivatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing shareholder returns through dividends and buybacks shows a positive allocation strategy.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, with expectations of approaching $1 billion for 2015.

evidence.text: The Company expects free cash flow for 2015 to approach $1 billion reflecting the impact of strong organic growth on working capital levels.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Expectation of free cash flow approaching $1 billion reflects strong cash generation.

factors.summary: Acquisition activity is strategic, focusing on consolidating the tool industry and expanding the Industrial platform.

evidence.text: The Company intends to pursue targets that consolidate the tool industry and expand the Industrial platform in Engineered Fastening and Infrastructure. The Company remains focused on improving the operating results of the Security business and plans to reevaluate its recovery progress and strategic fit by the second half of 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is strategically acquiring to consolidate and expand, indicating growth opportunities.

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share, particularly in the Tools & Storage segment.

evidence.text: The combination of these businesses is consistent with the Company's strategy to continue to gain market share and consolidate the tools industry. The Company classifies its business into three reportable segments, which also represent its operating segments: Tools & Storage, Security and Industrial.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is gaining market share, particularly in Tools & Storage, showing competitive strength.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are mixed, with growth in some regions offset by challenges in others.

evidence.text: Tools & Storage net sales increased 2% due to continued organic growth of 9%, driven by strong growth across all regions, partially offset by foreign currency pressure of 7%. Security net sales decreased 8% due to foreign currency declines of 7% and a slight decrease in organic growth of 1%, as lower volumes in emerging markets more than offset 4% organic growth in Europe.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed economic conditions with some growth offset by challenges, leading to a stable outlook.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are creating headwinds, particularly in the Security and Industrial segments.

evidence.text: Security net sales decreased 8% due to foreign currency declines of 7% and a slight decrease in organic growth of 1%, as lower volumes in emerging markets more than offset 4% organic growth in Europe. Industrial net sales declined 7% compared to the third quarter of 2014 as a result of a 7% decrease due to foreign currency pressures.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange pressures are causing significant headwinds in key segments, impacting performance.
